note: Directionless, range-bound tape — the worst environment for breakout (whipsaw / fakeouts). Exposure cut hard (multiplier 0.4); trade only the single cleanest, volume-confirmed breakout small, hold a large USDT reserve.

computed.regime_reason: breadth_pct 50 in [45,55] AND |median_24h_return| 0.1 < 1 AND fear_greed 49 neutral -> chop (directionless range)
computed.risk_budget_reason: (100 - target_stable_reserve 12) * 0.4 = 35.2; only the single cleanest confirmed breakout is taken small, the rest is USDT reserve to avoid whipsaw.

breakout_scores.components: the one name with an aligned stack AND a confirmed breakout (volume_ratio 1.6) in an otherwise flat tape
flat EMA stack, no new high, volume_ratio 1.0 (unconfirmed) -> below entry bar
EMA stack tangled, macd_hist<0, volume_ratio 0.8 -> no breakout

computed.breakout_reason: In chop almost everything fails confirmation. Only CAKE clears 0.65 with an aligned stack and a volume-backed Donchian breakout; it is taken at reduced size, and the rest of the book sits in the USDT reserve to avoid fakeout whipsaw.

entry: Enter only assets with breakout_score >= 0.65 AND a volume-confirmed Donchian breakout; in chop cap entries to the 1-2 cleanest names and size down.
Reject any breakout with volume_ratio < 1.5 (fakeout-prone in a range).
Per-name weight capped at 17%; keep the bulk of the book in the USDT reserve.
exit: Trim/exit when breakout_score < 0.50 on the next rebalance (the breakout failed back into the range).
Tighten the ATR trailing stop in chop; exit immediately on a close back inside the Donchian channel.
Force-exit at stop_loss 12% or take_profit 40%; rotate to USDT on a risk_off transition.

actions.reason: breakout_score 0.68; the only confirmed breakout in a flat tape, taken small
small starter only; volume unconfirmed so kept well under the cap
